{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the linear program in \\(\\mathbf{R}^2\\):\n\\[\n\\min x_1 \\quad \\text{subject to} \\quad x_1+x_2=1, \\qquad (x_1,x_2)\\ge 0.\n\\]\nShow that its primal-dual solution is\n\\[\nx^*=\\begin{pmatrix}0\\\\1\\end{pmatrix}, \\qquad \\lambda^*=0, \\qquad s^*=\\begin{pmatrix}1\\\\0\\end{pmatrix}.\n\\]\nAlso verify that the system \\(F(x,\\lambda,s)=0\\) has the spurious solution\n\\[\nx=\\begin{pmatrix}1\\\\0\\end{pmatrix}, \\qquad \\lambda=1, \\qquad s=\\begin{pmatrix}0\\\\-1\\end{pmatrix},\n\\]\nwhich is unrelated to the solution of the linear program. This illustrates that the bounds \\((x,s)\\ge 0\\) are essential for relating solutions of the system \\((14.4a)\\) to solutions of the linear program \\((14.1)\\) and its dual."}
